name: F.N.B. Corporation

profile.description: F.N.B. Corporation is a diversified financial services company headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. Operates through First National Bank, the largest subsidiary, providing commercial banking, consumer banking, insurance, and wealth management services across Pennsylvania, Ohio, Maryland, West Virginia, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Virginia. Approximately $47B in total assets. Founded in 1864.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): FNB launched the eStore Common Application platform — an AI-powered digital banking product that automates personalized recommendations and account opening — winning Best Digital Initiative from Fintech Futures [2]. Meaningful for a regional bank but not a transformative business model shift. AI Deployment (1/2): Generative AI is 'live in the bank' per CEO Delie, and FNB hired a Director of AI and Innovation (Santosh Sinha) and Director of Data Science (Sundeep Tangirala) in 2025 to expand AI capabilities [2]. The eStore platform uses AI and a data warehouse to deliver personalized recommendations, though no specific dollar savings have been quantified. Market Share (1/2): FNB is a leading regional bank in Pennsylvania with ~$47B assets, gaining share through organic growth and disciplined expansion into the Carolinas, though it remains small relative to money-center banks [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): eStore Business platform expansion, continued AI integration across service lines, and digital-first strategy with specific delivery timelines [2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Banks do not report R&D as a separate line item; no above-average technology spending data available relative to banking peers.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): FNB's core values — Integrity and Honor, Excellence and Passion, Teamwork and Collaboration, Innovation and Creativity — are clearly articulated and referenced in strategy, but are generic enough to apply to any bank [2]. The mission focuses on client experience and community banking without articulating a distinctive aspirational vision. Community Impact (1/2): FNB earned Coalition Greenwich's recognition as one of only six banks nationally for ESG commitment, and maintains community reinvestment programs across its footprint [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): Named Top Workplace USA for five consecutive years with 70+ workplace and cultural excellence awards since 2021, suggesting above-average employee satisfaction [2]. Insider Buying (0/2): Net insider selling observed — Chief Credit Officer Guerrieri sold 19,000 shares at $18.53 in recent months, and no meaningful net insider buying was identified [4].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 revenue grew 9.4% YoY driving 18.8% EPS growth to $0.38 per share, with return on tangible common equity of 13.2% and tangible book value growth of 11% [1]. D/E of 48% scores 1.0 (well below 70% target). ROE of 8.85% scores 0.59 (below 15% target, typical for regional banks). Revenue growth YoY of 9.4% scores 0.98. 5yr CAGR of 6.5% scores 0.77. Forward P/E of ~11 scores 1.0. Profit margin of 34% scores 1.0 [1]. Payout ratio of 30.2% scores 1.0. 41 consecutive years paid scores 1.0 (2x weight). However, consecutive years of dividend growth = 0 (flat dividend for 5 years) scores 0.0, and 5yr dividend growth CAGR of 0% scores 0.0 (2x weight) [4]. Weighted sum 8.34/12 = 6.95, rounded to 7. Liquidity: ~3M avg volume x $18.36 = $55M daily.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): Regional banking faces moderate AI disruption risk. Core lending, deposit-taking, and relationship banking activities are protected by regulatory requirements (FDIC, OCC oversight), switching costs, and the trust-intensive nature of commercial banking relationships [1]. However, fintech competitors and AI-powered lending platforms (SoFi, Upstart) are compressing margins in consumer lending, and AI chatbots threaten traditional branch-based service models. FNB's eStore platform represents proactive adaptation but the competitive moat is narrower than money-center banks. AI Opportunity (1/1): FNB has generative AI 'live in the bank,' the eStore platform uses AI for personalized product recommendations, and dedicated AI leadership hires (Director of AI, Director of Data Science) signal meaningful investment in AI-driven revenue generation and cost efficiency [2].

infinite_game.summary: FNB under CEO Delie operates as a well-managed regional bank focused on disciplined organic growth, but lacks the visionary purpose and transformative flexibility that define infinite game players. The company optimizes for consistent quarterly performance and incremental innovation rather than pursuing a bold vision of what banking could become. Sinek would view FNB as a competent finite player — reliably executing within the established banking framework without challenging the fundamental assumptions of the industry.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): FNB's values (Integrity, Excellence, Teamwork, Innovation) are well-articulated and genuinely lived — evidenced by 70+ workplace awards and Coalition Greenwich ESG recognition [2][3]. However, these are operational principles, not a Just Cause. There is no compelling vision of a future state that transcends the current business model. The company exists to be a good bank, not to transform banking or advance a broader purpose.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): CEO Delie made a forward-looking bet on digital banking with the eStore platform and AI investments when many regional peers were still branch-centric, investing in technology capabilities that won industry recognition [2]. The $250M share repurchase authorization shows capital allocation confidence. However, no evidence of walking away from profitable but misaligned business lines or making costly principled stands.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Five consecutive Top Workplace USA designations and 70+ awards since 2021 suggest a generally positive work environment with consistent leadership under Delie's tenure [2]. Newsweek's America's Most Admired Workplaces recognition reinforces this. However, no specific data on voluntary turnover rates, average tenure, or employee empowerment practices that would evidence exceptional trust.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): FNB has evolved from a traditional community bank to a technology-forward regional institution, launching eStore, hiring AI leadership, and expanding its digital capabilities [2]. The geographic expansion from Pennsylvania into the Carolinas represents strategic evolution. However, these are incremental adaptations within the existing banking model, not transformative reinvention.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): CEO Delie's competitive framing tends toward 'smaller banks coming out with innovative tools that larger banks miss' — positioning FNB as winning against larger rivals rather than learning from them [2]. No evidence of management publicly acknowledging competitors' strengths or using rival performance as motivation for self-improvement.

investment_thesis: FNB delivers consistent regional banking performance with Q1 2026 showing 9.4% revenue growth, 18.8% EPS growth to $0.38, and return on tangible equity of 13.2% [1]. The $47B-asset bank trades at an attractive 11x forward P/E with a 2.67% dividend yield and conservative 30% payout ratio, providing both income and multiple expansion potential. CEO Delie's technology-forward strategy — eStore platform, AI integration, dedicated data science leadership — positions FNB ahead of regional banking peers on digital transformation [2]. Full-year NII guidance of $1.50-1.54B and positive operating leverage of 5% signal continued earnings momentum through 2026.
thesis_continued: The bear case reflects structural challenges facing regional banking. ROE of 8.85% is well below the 15% target, typical for banks operating in a compressed net interest margin environment, and the stock's price-to-book of 0.96 reflects the market's skepticism about sustained profitability [1]. The dividend has been flat for five consecutive years ($0.12/quarter) despite 41 years of payments, signaling management's reluctance or inability to grow shareholder returns [4]. Fintech disruption from AI-powered lending platforms threatens FNB's consumer lending margins, and the bank remains exposed to regional economic concentration in Pennsylvania and Ohio. Insider selling (CCO sold 19K shares) rather than buying suggests limited near-term conviction from management.
key_catalysts: AI-powered eStore platform expansion into business banking, with potential to drive non-interest income growth and deepen client relationships across the $47B asset base
Full-year 2026 NII guidance of $1.50-1.54B with 5% positive operating leverage, positioning for potential dividend increase after five years of flat payments
Share repurchase program of $250M+ providing price support and EPS accretion, alongside potential M&A opportunities in fragmented regional banking
key_risks: ROE of 8.85% structurally below cost of equity for regional banks, with price-to-book of 0.96 reflecting market doubt about ability to generate adequate returns on capital
Five consecutive years of flat dividend payments ($0.12/quarter) despite growing earnings, creating a negative signal for income-focused investors
AI-powered fintech competitors (SoFi, Upstart) compressing consumer lending margins and threatening the branch-based relationship banking model that underpins FNB's regional advantage
